name: Una conversación que lo cambió todo

sentences.base_language: A conversation that changed everything
sentences.target_language: Una conversación que lo cambió todo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Lo cambió todo** = it changed everything — **lo** here is a neuter direct object pronoun referring to the abstract idea (everything about the situation).
**Cambiar** = to change — regular -ar verb; **cambió** is the preterite third-person singular.

sentences.base_language: It was a rainy Thursday when I met her
sentences.target_language: Era un jueves lluvioso cuando la conocí
sentences.grammarNote:
**Era** = it was — imperfecto (imperfect) of **ser**, used for setting the scene/background.
**Conocí** = I met — preterite of **conocer** (to know/meet for the first time); the preterite marks a single completed event.
Note the contrast: imperfecto **era** (background) vs preterite **conocí** (the event that happened).

sentences.base_language: I was waiting for the bus when she sat down next to me
sentences.target_language: Yo estaba esperando el autobús cuando ella se sentó a mi lado
sentences.grammarNote:
**Estaba esperando** = I was waiting — imperfecto of **estar** + gerund (**-ando**): the ongoing background action.
**Se sentó** = she sat down — preterite (completed action) interrupting the ongoing waiting.
This is the classic imperfecto/preterite contrast: **estaba** (ongoing) interrupted by **se sentó** (event).

sentences.base_language: She looked like she had been crying
sentences.target_language: Parecía que había estado llorando
sentences.grammarNote:
**Parecía** = she seemed/looked — imperfecto of **parecer** (to seem).
**Había estado llorando** = had been crying — pluperfect progressive: **haber** (imperfecto) + **estado** + gerund.
This is the past perfect progressive, indicating an action completed before another past moment.

sentences.base_language: I didn't know whether to say something or not
sentences.target_language: No sabía si decir algo o no
sentences.grammarNote:
**Sabía** = I knew / I didn't know — imperfecto of **saber** (to know facts/how to do things), expressing a mental state in the past.
**Si + infinitive** = whether to do something: **no sabía si hablar** (I didn't know whether to speak).
Contrast **si** (whether/if) with **sí** (yes — with accent).

sentences.base_language: In the end I asked if she was alright
sentences.target_language: Al final le pregunté si estaba bien
sentences.grammarNote:
**Al final** = in the end, finally — **al** is the contraction of **a + el**.
**Le pregunté** = I asked her — **le** is the indirect object pronoun (to her); **pregunté** is the preterite.
**Si estaba bien** = if/whether she was alright — **si** + imperfecto for indirect questions about past states.

sentences.base_language: She laughed a little and said she wasn't sure
sentences.target_language: Ella se rio un poco y dijo que no estaba segura
sentences.grammarNote:
**Se rio** = she laughed — preterite of **reírse** (reflexive), irregular: **se rio** (not *se rió* — no accent needed when the stem is monosyllabic).
**Dijo que** = she said that — **decir** (to say) is irregular in the preterite: **dije, dijiste, dijo**.
**No estaba segura** = she wasn't sure — imperfecto for a past state of uncertainty.

sentences.base_language: We started talking and I couldn't believe how natural it felt
sentences.target_language: Empezamos a hablar y no podía creer lo natural que resultó
sentences.grammarNote:
**Empezamos a hablar** = we started talking — **empezar a + infinitive** = to start to do something.
**No podía creer** = I couldn't believe — imperfecto of **poder** for an ongoing inability.
**Lo natural que resultó** — **lo + adjective + que** intensifies: *how natural it turned out to be*. **Resultar** = to turn out.

sentences.base_language: She told me that she had just quit her job
sentences.target_language: Me dijo que acababa de renunciar a su trabajo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me dijo** = she told me — preterite of **decir** (irregular: dijo).
**Acababa de + infinitive** = had just done something — imperfecto of **acabar de** shifts *have just* into the past.
**Renunciar a** = to quit, to resign from — always followed by **a**: **renunciar al trabajo** (resign from the job).

sentences.base_language: I was surprised that she didn't seem sad about it
sentences.target_language: Me sorprendió que no pareciera triste por eso
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me sorprendió** = it surprised me — preterite of **sorprender** (works like **gustar**: the surprising thing is the subject).
**Que no pareciera** — after expressions of emotion/reaction in the past, the subjunctive imperfecto (**pareciera**) is used.
**Por eso** = about that / because of that — **por** expresses cause.

sentences.base_language: She said she hoped it would turn out to be the best decision of her life
sentences.target_language: Dijo que esperaba que resultara ser la mejor decisión de su vida
sentences.grammarNote:
**Dijo que esperaba** — sequence of tenses: main verb in preterite (**dijo**) → reported speech in imperfecto (**esperaba**).
**Esperaba que + subjunctive imperfecto** (**resultara**) — after **esperar que** in the past, use the imperfecto subjunctive.
**La mejor decisión** = the best decision — **mejor** is the superlative of **bueno/bien**: **el/la mejor**.

sentences.base_language: I told her I admired that she had dared to make such a drastic change
sentences.target_language: Le dije que admiraba que se hubiera atrevido a hacer un cambio tan drástico
sentences.grammarNote:
**Le dije** = I told her — **le** (indirect object: to her) + **dije** (preterite of **decir**).
**Se hubiera atrevido** = had dared — pluperfect subjunctive: **haber** (imperfecto subj.) + past participle.
**Atreverse a + infinitive** (reflexive) = to dare to do something: **me atrevo a hablar** (I dare to speak).

sentences.base_language: She asked me if I had ever wanted to do something like that
sentences.target_language: Me preguntó si alguna vez había querido hacer algo así
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me preguntó si** = she asked me if/whether — indirect question using **si**.
**Alguna vez** = ever, at any time — placed before or after the verb: **¿has ido alguna vez?** (have you ever been?)
**Había querido** = had wanted — pluperfect: **haber** (imperfecto) + past participle **querido**.

sentences.base_language: I thought about it and realized that I was afraid of taking risks
sentences.target_language: Lo pensé y me di cuenta de que tenía miedo de arriesgarme
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me di cuenta de que** = I realized that — **darse cuenta de** is a reflexive phrase meaning *to realize*.
**Tenía miedo de** = I was afraid of — **tener miedo** (to be afraid) is a **tener + noun** expression; imperfecto for a background state.
**Arriesgarme** = taking risks / risking myself — reflexive **arriesgarse** (to risk oneself).

sentences.base_language: But I didn't tell her that
sentences.target_language: Pero no le dije eso
sentences.grammarNote:
**No le dije eso** = I didn't tell her that — **le** (indirect object: to her), **dije** (preterite of **decir**), **eso** (that — neuter demonstrative pronoun).
Note: in Spanish, indirect object pronouns (**le**, **me**, **te**) must appear even when the person is named: **no le dije a ella eso**.

sentences.base_language: I said I was happy with the choices I had made so far
sentences.target_language: Dije que estaba contento con las decisiones que había tomado hasta ahora
sentences.grammarNote:
**Dije que estaba contento** — sequence of tenses: preterite **dije** → imperfecto **estaba** (reported speech in the past).
**Había tomado** = had made — pluperfect for an action completed before another past moment.
**Hasta ahora** = up until now, so far — **hasta** (until/up to) + **ahora** (now).

sentences.base_language: She gave me a look as if she didn't quite believe me
sentences.target_language: Me miró como si no me creyera del todo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Como si + subjunctive imperfecto** = as if — always triggers the imperfecto subjunctive: **como si fuera** (as if it were), **como si no creyera** (as if she didn't believe).
**Del todo** = quite, entirely — used in negatives: **no me creyera del todo** (didn't quite/entirely believe me).

sentences.base_language: Maybe she was right
sentences.target_language: Quizás tenía razón
sentences.grammarNote:
**Quizás** = maybe, perhaps — can take indicative or subjunctive depending on certainty.
**Quizás** + indicative (**tenía**) = slightly more certain (maybe she was right).
**Quizás** + subjunctive (**tuviera**) = more doubtful. Here the indicative suggests the narrator half-believes it.
**Tener razón** = to be right (literally *to have reason*) — a **tener + noun** expression.

sentences.base_language: We talked until our buses came
sentences.target_language: Hablamos hasta que llegaron nuestros autobuses
sentences.grammarNote:
**Hasta que** = until — followed by indicative when referring to a completed past event: **hablamos hasta que llegaron** (we talked until they arrived).
Contrast: **hasta que** + subjunctive when referring to a future/hypothetical event: **hablaré hasta que lleguen** (I'll talk until they arrive).
**Llegaron** = they arrived — preterite of **llegar** (regular -ar verb in preterite).

sentences.base_language: Before leaving she told me not to waste any more time
sentences.target_language: Antes de irse me dijo que no perdiera más tiempo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Antes de irse** = before leaving — **antes de + infinitive**; the reflexive **irse** (to leave/go away) stays as infinitive.
**Me dijo que no perdiera** — reported command in the past: **decir que + subjunctive imperfecto** (**perdiera**) conveys an instruction or request.
**Perder tiempo** = to waste time (literally *to lose time*).

sentences.base_language: I never saw her again but I think about that conversation often
sentences.target_language: Nunca la volví a ver pero pienso en esa conversación a menudo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Nunca la volví a ver** = I never saw her again — **volver a + infinitive** = to do something again: **volver a ver** (to see again). Negated with **nunca**: I never saw her again.
**Pienso en** = I think about — **pensar en** (to think about) takes the preposition **en**.
**A menudo** = often, frequently — a set adverbial phrase.

sentences.base_language: It was as if she had said exactly what I needed to hear
sentences.target_language: Fue como si hubiera dicho exactamente lo que yo necesitaba escuchar
sentences.grammarNote:
**Fue como si** + pluperfect subjunctive — **como si** (as if) with a past-before-past meaning uses the pluperfect subjunctive: **hubiera dicho** (had said).
**Lo que yo necesitaba** = what I needed — **lo que** is the neuter relative pronoun (*that which / what*).
**Necesitaba** = I needed — imperfecto for an ongoing past need.

sentences.base_language: Six months later I finally quit my job too
sentences.target_language: Seis meses después yo también renuncié a mi trabajo por fin
sentences.grammarNote:
**Seis meses después** = six months later — **después** as an adverb of time, without **de** when not followed by a noun.
**Yo también** = I too / I also — **también** (also) for affirmative agreement.
**Por fin** = finally, at last — more emphatic and emotional than **finalmente**: ¡Por fin lo hice!

sentences.base_language: I still don't know if it was a coincidence or if that stranger planted a seed in me
sentences.target_language: Todavía no sé si fue una coincidencia o si aquella extraña plantó una semilla en mí
sentences.grammarNote:
**Todavía no** = still not / not yet — **todavía** emphasizes the ongoing nature of not knowing.
**Aquella extraña** = that stranger (feminine, that over there) — **aquel/aquella** refers to something more remote (in time or space) than **ese/esa**.
**Plantó una semilla** = planted a seed — figurative use; **plantar** is a regular -ar verb in the preterite.
